我写了一份 750 页的自托管生产应用程序指南

I wrote a 750-page guide to self-hosting production apps

selfdeployment.io 作者 kocyigityunus 分数 55 评论 12 2026/03/23 21:35
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 目录常见问题解答关于作者通过这本深入实用的掌握 Kubernetes 和生产基础设施的指南，了解如何在自己的基础设施上部署和运行生产应用程序。750 页·超过 35 个代码块·无 AI Slop 本书内容作为软件开发人员，在过去十年中，我们越来越依赖云平台和托管服务。甚至如此，我们几乎忘记了如何部署我们自己的应用程序。随着系统复杂性不断增加和软件质量下降，这种依赖也会影响我们理解系统和创建可靠解决方案的能力。软件开发人员的自我部署可以弥补这些知识差距，并帮助您了解整个系统，以便您在自己的基础设施上部署软件。为了实现这一目标，它从基础知识开始，并在此基础上进行构建，展示您在实践中会遇到的真实示例。在 750 页和 40 多个代码块（总计超过 2,500 行）中，您将从使用基本 Linux 命令逐步发展到创建 Docker 化应用程序、部署 Kubernetes 集群、扩展 PostgreSQL 数据库，最终了解多节点集群的每个组件。这包括负载均衡、TLS、CI/CD 管道和操作最佳实践。它专为以下开发人员而设计：+想要一份可以真正重读的参考资料，而不仅仅是一个只需遵循一次的教程+厌倦了为托管服务付费，您可以自己运行自己的一小部分成本，同时保持对基础设施的完全控制+编写后端或完整堆栈并希望拥有整个部署过程+复制粘贴 Kubernetes 清单但没有真正理解它们+更喜欢从实际生产场景中学习而不是琐碎的内容书内示例从 Linux 基础到生产级 Kubernetes 的完整路线图。59271564语言英语作者 Yunus Koçyiğit 长度 750 页技术内容 · 约 30 小时的阅读时间代码示例 35+ 个实用示例，带有约 2,000 行带注释的代码主题 Linux、Docker、网络、TLS、存储、Kubernetes、Helm、CI/CD 和实际最佳实践格式 PDF · 针对屏幕技术进行了优化阅读难度中级 - 需要基本编程知识最新更新✓ 2026 年 3 月拥有您的 INFRAZERO 供应商锁定定价支付您想要的，获得一切，并立即下载完整的软件包。支付您想要的完整软件包起价 0 美元，如果这本书对您有帮助，请回来付款。建议价格 19 美元是支持工作的可靠方式。+完整的 750 页 PDF 手册+35+ 生产就绪代码块+按照自己的节奏学习+完整的基础设施堆栈覆盖+结构化搜索、参考和人工智能辅助+复制粘贴基础设施模板+作者的直接电子邮件支持+真实世界的部署示例+编写以节省您数周的试验和错误获取这本书关于作者Yunus Koçyiğit 是一位全堆栈软件工程师，热衷于创建简单而可靠的系统。获得计算机科学学士学位后，他开始了移动开发人员的职业生涯，帮助各种规模的公司构建应用程序，其中包括欧盟最大的银行法国巴黎富通银行 (BNP Paribas Fortis)。在他的整个职业生涯中，他所从事的项目拥有整个开发生命周期，包括部署、CI/CD 管道、监控和基础设施。这段现实世界的经历塑造了他对开发人员在生产中运行软件实际需要什么的理解，本书将这些经验教训汇总成一本实用指南。2021 年，他创立了一家初创公司，该公司建立了一个租赁广告空间的市场并筹集了天使投资，但未能实现产品与市场的契合。他目前在 Cyber​​Whiz 工作，这是一家网络安全公司，帮助企业实现 CRA 合规性。除了工作之外，他还喜欢烹饪食物、修理损坏的东西（电子产品、汽车等）以及与妻子和 1 岁的儿子共度时光。请随时通过 yunus@kcygt.io 与他联系。常见问题解答有关本书的常见问题。© 2026 Yunus Koçyiğitv1.1.2

Original Summary

  1. Table of ContentsFAQAbout the AuthorLearn how to deploy and run production applications on your own infrastructure with this deep, practical guide to mastering Kubernetes and production infrastructure.750 pages · 35+ code blocks · No AI SlopWhat This Book is AboutAs software developers, we have become increasingly dependent on cloud platforms and managed services in the last decade. Even so much so that we nearly forgot how to deploy our own applications. This reliance also affects our ability to understand the system and create reliable solutions, as system complexity continues to increase and software quality decreases.Self-deployment for Software Developers addresses these knowledge gaps and helps you understand the system as a whole to let you deploy software on your own infrastructure. To achieve this, it starts with the basics and builds on them, showing real-world examples as you will encounter them in practice.Across 750 pages and more than 40 code blocks (totaling more than 2,500 lines), you’ll progress from using essential Linux commands to creating a Dockerized application, deploying a Kubernetes cluster, extending PostgreSQL database, ultimately understanding each component of a multi-node cluster. This includes load balancing, TLS, CI/CD pipelines, and operational best practices.It's designed for developers who:+Want a reference you can actually re-read, not just a tutorial to follow once+Are tired of paying for managed services you could run yourself for a fraction of the cost while keeping full control over your infrastructure+Write backend or full-stack and want to own the whole deployment process+Have copy-pasted Kubernetes manifests without really understanding them+Prefer learning from real production scenarios over trivial examplesInside The BookA complete roadmap from Linux foundations to production-grade Kubernetes.59271564LanguageEnglishAuthorYunus KoçyiğitLength750 pages of technical content · ~30 hours of reading timeCode examples35+ practical examples with ~2,000 lines of annotated codeTopicsLinux, Docker, networking, TLS, storage, Kubernetes, Helm, CI/CD, and real-world best practicesFormatPDF · optimized for on-screen technical readingDifficultyIntermediate - Basic programming knowledge requiredLatest Update✓ March 2026OWN YOUR INFRAZERO VENDOR LOCK-INPricingPay what you want, get everything, and download the complete package today.Pay What You WantComplete PackageStarting at$0Come back and pay if the book helped you.Suggested Price$19A solid way to support the work.+Complete 750 pages PDF manual+35+ production-ready code blocks+Learn at your own pace+Full infrastructure stack coverage+Structured for search, reference, and AI assistance+Copy-paste infrastructure templates+Direct email support from the author+Real world deployment examples+Written to save you weeks of trial and errorGet the BookAbout the AuthorYunus Koçyiğit is a full-stack software engineer with a passion for creating simple and reliable systems. After getting his Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, he started his career as a mobile developer and helped building applications for companies at various sizes, including BNP Paribas Fortis, the largest bank in the European Union.Throughout his career, he has worked on projects where he owned the entire development lifecycle, including deployment, CI/CD pipelines, monitoring, and infrastructure. That real-world experience shaped his understanding of what developers actually need to run software in production, and this book brings those lessons together into a practical guide.In 2021, he founded a startup that built a marketplace for renting advertising space and raised angel investment, but it failed to reach product-market fit. He currently works for CyberWhiz, a cyber security company that helps businesses achieve their CRA compliance.Aside from his work, he loves cooking food, fixing broken things (electronics, cars, etc.) and spending time with his wife and 1 year old son. Feel free to contact him at yunus@kcygt.io.FAQAnswers to common questions about the book.© 2026 Yunus Koçyiğitv1.1.2

评论（默认中文）

kocyigityunus 2026/03/23 21:35
我已经为此工作了近两年。我原本打算在我儿子出生之前完成它，但他现在已经 16 个月大了。去算算吧。写书似乎比看起来更难。我最初的动机是写一些我想读的东西，因为我在自托管上找到的大多数资源要么太浅薄，要么缺乏代码等现实世界的示例，要么没有完全解决我不断遇到的知识差距。

本书从基础知识开始，逐步覆盖完整的基础设施堆栈，目标是从整体上理解系统并最终在 Kubernetes 上进行部署。 Kubernetes 是一个主要焦点，但内容可以应用于任何环境。我无法清楚地表达这一点，但您可能应该检查示例“作业和 CronJobs”部分以获得一个想法。还有一个部分介绍了最佳实践、技巧和基于我自己遇到的事情的实用细节。

它是免费提供的，包括 PDF 和代码块。然而，我们欢迎您支付您想要的费用。

I’ve been working on this for nearly two years. I originally aimed to complete it before my son was born, but he’s now 16 months old. Go figure. Seems like writing a book is harder than it looks. My original motivation was to write something I would want to read since most resources I found on self-hosting were either too shallow, lacked real-world examples like code, or didn’t fully address the knowledge gaps I kept running into.

The book starts with the basics and builds up to covering the full infrastructure stack, with the goal of understanding the system as a whole and eventually deploying on Kubernetes. Kubernetes is a major focus but the content can be applied to any environment. I can't express this clearly, but you should probably check the sample "Jobs and CronJobs" section to get an idea. There’s also a section on best practices, tips, and practical details based on things I’ve run into myself.

It is available for free including the PDF and the code blocks. Yet, you are welcome to pay what you want.

Thank you. It was the hardest thing I've ever did by far.

eviks 2026/03/24 09:40
> 针对屏幕技术阅读而优化的 PDF

PDF 没有对此进行优化，就像现在，在手机上阅读文章时，我无法正确查看某一章，因为 PDF 在针对较小屏幕进行自身优化方面很糟糕

> PDF optimized for on-screen technical reading

PDF isn't optimized for that, like now, reading the article a phone, I couldn't properly check out a chapter because PDF is awful in optimizing itself for a smaller screen

Thank you so much for such a useful book, I really appreciate what you did! But please add alternative payment methods to thank you, I don't trust leaving credit card details on third-party services

kocyigityunus 2026/03/24 12:19
我与几家出版商进行了交谈。如今，一本 750 页的高质量印刷书似乎在经济上不可行。
I talked with several publishers. It seems a 750-page, high-quality printed book just isn’t economically viable these days.
